zh.lifeSummary: 當Rhoda A Hancock於1787年3月1日出生在美國維吉尼亞州時，她的父親William L. Hancock26歲，母親Sarah Harding22歲。她於1803年與John Tresenriter在美國肯塔基州謝佛遜結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1849年4月11日在美國印第安納州弗洛伊德格奧智托 堂史普格奧智托享年62歲，埋葬在美國印第安納州弗洛伊德格奧智托 堂史普格奧智托米納德 比爾德 法姆 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Rhoda A Hancock于1787年3月1日出生在美国弗吉尼亚州时，她的父亲William L. Hancock26岁，母亲Sarah Harding22岁。她于1803年与John Tresenriter在美国肯塔基州謝佛遜结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1849年4月11日在美国印第安纳州弗洛伊德格奧智托 堂史普格奧智托享年62岁，埋葬在美国印第安纳州弗洛伊德格奧智托 堂史普格奧智托米納德 比爾德 法姆 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Hancock
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Hann + the hypocoristic suffix -cok, which was commonly added to personal names (see Cocke ).
Dutch: from Middle Dutch hanecoc ‘winkle, periwinkle’ (a type of shellfish), probably a metonymic occupational name for someone who gathered and sold shellfish.

History: Thomas Hancock, the uncle of Declaration of Independence signatory John Hancock (1736/7–93), was among the foremost of 18th-century American businessmen. He was a descendant of Nathaniel Hancock, who was known to have been in Cambridge, MA, as early as 1634. Born in Braintree, MA, John Hancock was president of the Second Continental Congress and the first governor of the state of MA.

variants.name: Hancox
meanings: English: variant of Hancock , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Handcock
meanings: English: variant of Hancock with intrusive -d-.

variants.name: Hann
meanings: German and Dutch: from a shortened pet form of the personal name Johann (see John ).

English: from the Middle English personal name Hann, commonly a pet form of Henry (Middle English Hanry). Hann could also be a rhyming pet form of Randolph (Middle English Randal, shortened to Ran or Rand). Han was also a pet form of Joh(a)n in Middle Dutch, and it is possible that this usage was brought to England by Flemings after the Norman Conquest, but no evidence has yet been found that it was similarly used in Middle English. See Henry and Randall , and compare Hankin , Hancock , and Hanks .
English: variant of Ann with prosthetic H-.

nameMeanings.name: Rhoda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the post-classical Greek name Rhoda, derived either from rhodon ‘rose’, or as an ethnic name meaning ‘woman from Rhodes’ (Greek Rhodos). In the New Testament Rhoda was a servant in the house of Mary the mother of John, where Peter went after his release from prison by an angel (Acts 12:13). In the Scottish Highlands Rhoda is used as a feminine form of Roderick .

historicEvents.title: The First Presidential Election
historicEvents.description: The First Presidential election was held in the newly created United States of America. Under the Articles of Confederation, the executive branch of the country was not set up for an individual to help lead the nation. So, under the United States Constitution they position was put in. Because of his prominent roles during the Revolutionary War, George Washington was voted in unanimously as the First President of the United States.

historicEvents.title: Becomes the 10th state
historicEvents.description: On June 25, 1788 Virginia became the 10th state.
Lewis and Clark set out from St. Louis, MO to explore the West.

historicEvents.title: Monumental Church Built
historicEvents.description: The Monumental Church was built between 1812-1814 on the sight where the Richmond Theatre fire had taken place. It is a monument to those that died in the fire.

historicEvents.title: Making States Equal
historicEvents.description: The Missouri Compromise helped provide the entrance of Maine as a free state and Missouri as a slave state into the United States. As part of the compromise, slavery was prohibited north of the 36°30′ parallel, excluding Missouri.

historicEvents.title: """Mary Randolph Publishes """"The Virginia Housewife"""""""
historicEvents.description: “The Virginia Housewife” was published by Mary Randolph. It was the first cookbook published in America.

historicEvents.title: Remember the Alamo
historicEvents.description: Being a monumental event in the Texas Revolution, The Battle of the Alamo was a thirteen-day battle at the Alamo Mission near San Antonio. In the early morning of the final battle, the Mexican Army advanced on the Alamo. Quickly being overrun, the Texian Soldiers quickly withdrew inside the building. The battle has often been overshadowed by events from the Mexican–American War, But the Alamo gradually became known as a national battle site and later named an official Texas State Shrine.

historicEvents.title: Lumpkin's Jail
historicEvents.description: In 1844 when Robert Lumpkin bought land in Virginia, this would be the spot of the Infamous Slave Jail (or Lumpkin’s Jail). The slaves would be brought here during the slave trade until they were sold. Lumpkin had purchased the land for his own slave business.

en.lifeSummary: When Rhoda A Hancock was born on 1 March 1787, in Virginia, United States, her father, William L. Hancock, was 26 and her mother, Sarah Harding, was 22. She married John Tresenriter in 1803, in Jefferson, Kentucky, United States. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 11 April 1849, in Georgetown, Georgetown Township, Floyd, Indiana, United States, at the age of 62, and was buried in Mynard Beard Farm Cemetery, Georgetown, Georgetown Township, Floyd, Indiana, United States.

Elle a épousé John Tresenriter en 1803, à Jefferson, Kentucky, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 2 filles. ja.lifeSummary: Rhoda A Hancockが1787年3月1日にアメリカ合衆国バージニア州で生まれた時，父William L. Hancockは26歳，母Sarah Hardingは22歳であった。1803年にアメリカ合衆国ケンタッキー州ジェフエルソンでJohn Tresenriterと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1849年4月11日にアメリカ合衆国インディアナ州フロイドジョージェトウン トウンシュイップジョージェトウンで62歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国インディアナ州フロイドジョージェトウン トウンシュイップジョージェトウンムイナルド ビールド ファルム セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Rhoda A Hancock(이)가 1787년 3월 1일 미국 버지니아 주에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William L. Hancock(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sarah Harding(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1803년 미국 켄터키 주 제퍼슨에서 John Tresenriter(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1849년 4월 11일 미국 인디애나 주 플로이드 조짓운 타운시프 조짓운에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 인디애나 주 플로이드 조짓운 타운시프 조짓운 마이나르드 브이르드 패 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Rhoda A Hancock nasceu em 1 de março de 1787, em Virgínia, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, William L. Hancock, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Sarah Harding, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com John Tresenriter em 1803, em Jefferson, Kentucky, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 11 de abril de 1849, em Georgetown, Georgetown Township, Floyd, Indiana, Estados Unidos da América, com 62 anos, e foi sepultada em Mynard Beard Farm Cemetery, Georgetown, Georgetown Township, Floyd, Indiana, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.Georgetown, Floyd, Indiana, United State: джеоргетоун, джеоргетоун тоуншип, флоид, Индиана, Соединенные штаты
ru.Mynard Beard Farm Cemetery, Georgetown,: минард бирд фарм кеметири, джеоргетоун, джеоргетоун тоуншип, флоид, Индиана, Соединенные штаты
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Rhoda A Hancock родилась 1 марта 1787 г. в местности Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты, её отцу по имени William L. Hancock было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Sarah Harding, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Tresenriter в 1803 г. в местности джеффирсон, Кентукки, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла 11 апреля 1849 г. в местности джеоргетоун, джеоргетоун тоуншип, флоид, Индиана, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 62 лет и была похоронена в местности минард бирд фарм кеметири, джеоргетоун, джеоргетоун тоуншип, флоид, Индиана, Соединенные штаты.

es.1 March 1787–11 April 1849: 1 de marzo de 1787–11 de abril de 1849
es.Georgetown, Floyd, Indiana, United State: Georgetown, Georgetown Township, Floyd, Indiana, Estados Unidos
es.Mynard Beard Farm Cemetery, Georgetown,: Mynard Beard Farm Cemetery, Georgetown, Georgetown Township, Floyd, Indiana, Estados Unidos
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Rhoda A Hancock nació el 1 de marzo de 1787, en Virginia, Estados Unidos, su papá, William L. Hancock, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Sarah Harding, tenía 22 años. Se casó con John Tresenriter en 1803, en Jefferson, Kentucky, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 11 de abril de 1849, en Georgetown, Georgetown Township, Floyd, Indiana, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultada en Mynard Beard Farm Cemetery, Georgetown, Georgetown Township, Floyd, Indiana, Estados Unidos.
